channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Regional Spanish leader who botched flood response faces another big march demanding his resignation
item.description: Tens of thousands of Spaniards have marched in the streets of Valencia a month after devastating floods to demand the resignation of the regional officials who bungled the emergency response.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Stealth destroyer to be home for 1st hypersonic weapon on a US warship
item.description: The U.S. Navy is transforming a costly flub into a potent weapon with the first shipborne hypersonic weapon, which is being retrofitted aboard the first of its three stealthy destroyers.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, as his intended nominee for ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy says NATO offer for Ukraine-controlled territory could end 'hot stage' of war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An offer of NATO membership to territory under Kyiv’s control would end “the hot stage of the war” in Ukraine, but any proposal to join the military alliance should be extended to all parts of the country that fall under internationally recognized borders, President Volodymyr Ze
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Israeli diplomat: Israel-Hamas ceasefire not likely before Biden leaves
item.description: Former Israeli diplomat Alon Pinkas predicted Saturday that a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza is unlikely before President Biden departs the White House early next year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky suggests NATO membership could halt 'hot phase’ of war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky suggested this week that NATO welcoming Ukraine into the alliance could halt the “hot stage” of the war, while Russia would temporarily keep the territory it overtook.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip killed five people including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg And Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After entering Aleppo, Syrian insurgents advance to a nearby province. Assad says he'll defeat them
item.description: Thousands of Syrian insurgents have taken over most of Aleppo, establishing positions in the country’s largest city and controlling its airport before expanding their shock offensive to a nearby province.
item.dc:creator: Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Bolivia's scrappy highlands, proud Indigenous Cholas take the runway by storm
item.description: VIACHA, Bolivia (AP) — In the huddled markets, sprawling farms and pulsing parties of Viacha, a town southeast of Bolivia’s capital, it's typical for women to sport bowler hats, tiered skirts and fringed shawls.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and JUAN KARITA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shock offensive on Syria's largest city
item.description: Syrian rebels against the country's President Bashar Al-Assad advanced toward one of the largest cities in the country — the first time since 2016 — following a shock offensive launched by insurgents on Wednesday, the Associated Press reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: What Trump's Russia-Ukraine envoy has said about ending the war
item.description: President-elect Trump's choice for special envoy to the Russia-Ukraine war, Keith Kellogg, has offered fairly detailed proposals for ending the war that began almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Enraged passenger unloads flurry of punches on airport worker, wild video shows
item.description: A furious airline passenger was captured on dramatic video unleashing a flurry of punches on an airport worker over an apparent check-in delay dispute.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: On a bright winter day, workers at a Ukrainian thermal power plant repair its heavily damaged equipment as drops of water from melted snow leak through gaping holes in its battered roof.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senior figure with Scholz's ex-allies quits over 'D-Day' paper on German government collapse
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A senior figure in the party whose departure from Chancellor Olaf Scholz's coalition three weeks ago put Germany on the road to an early election resigned Friday in a furor over an internal document discussing how the party could leave the government, a presentation whose title referre
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After more than five years of frenetic, but sometimes interrupted, reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral showed itself anew to the world Friday, with rebuilt soaring ceilings and creamy good-as-new stonework erasing somber memories of its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy ordered missile strikes into Russia hours after trip to front lines with Fox News
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy recently discussed the latest developments with his country's war against Russia with Fox News chief foreign correspondent Trey Yingst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Canadian hiker found alive after spending weeks in blistering cold
item.description: A missing hiker was located in the Canadian wilderness after being lost for more than six weeks, according to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 15 after landslides bury 40 homes in eastern Uganda
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — At least 15 people have died and 113 others are missing after landslides buried homes in six villages in eastern Uganda, police said Thursday. Another 15 injured people have been rescued and admitted to Buluganya Health Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel opens fire in Lebanon at ‘suspects’ allegedly violating truce, which has entered its second day
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces opened fire Thursday on "suspects" in southern Lebanon who allegedly are violating the terms of the ceasefire deal with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Israel ramps up strikes on Beirut as its leadership prepares to vote on ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military also issued warnings for 20 more buildings in Beirut's suburbs to evacuate before they too were struck
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation
